human_verification.notes: Reviewed KYC-specific onboarding boundaries, source quality, identity and beneficial-ownership evidence handling, access and go-live gating, fraud or AML escalation posture, and scenario-to-ability coverage.

review_notes: All required financial-services source classes and required scenario families are represented in the research summary.
All shared, onboarding-phase, and financial-services-and-insurance abilities are present in the functionality map and covered by passing scenarios.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because product, jurisdiction, and tenant-policy differences are explicit runtime retrieval and escalation dependencies.

specialist_owned_evidence: Financial-services-specific onboarding evidence includes CIP and CDD records, beneficial-owner certifications, sanctions-screening results, account or policy setup traces, access approvals, and deferred-item handoff state rather than generic onboarding paperwork.
Boundary handling explicitly stops at readiness validation and handoff packaging; underwriting, sanctions clearance, legal interpretation, and final AML decisions remain outside the lane.
Go-live claims require evidence-backed prerequisite closure, explicit blocker capture, and named next-owner routing.
notes: Runtime remains `specification-based` and does not claim a retained-implementation implementation unless explicitly promoted in a separate change.
